items.claim: Alberto Montes has a height and reach disadvantage against Tommy McKillan.
items.quote: Montes, even though he does flow nice, he's given up some height and some reach here. Um, McKillan's six feet at featherweight, whereas Montes is 5'7.

items.claim: Tommy McKillan maintains a high pace and can fight effectively even when tired.
items.quote: McKillan has shown me that he could go for a crazy pace. His endurance doesn't like fully like he doesn't fully give in on himself.

items.claim: Alberto Montes has not been tested in a high-pace war setting.
items.quote: Where Montes, I haven't really seen him in that war setting.

items.claim: Tommy McKillan is prone to being overzealous and chasing finishes.
items.quote: I think McKillan just gets a little bit overhappy sometimes and chases a finish like when he's overzealous.

items.claim: Mitch Ramirez is on a three-fight losing streak in the UFC.
items.quote: He's currently running a three-ight losing streak.

items.claim: Chase Hooper has poor defensive striking.
items.quote: Hooper striking looks good sometimes, but most of the time his defensive striking looks horrible and abysmal.

items.claim: Chase Hooper is highly susceptible to being knocked out.
Jared Cannonier's chin is a significant durability concern.
items.quote: Of course, his last couple fights, like his chin's always the biggest worry.

items.claim: CLD has shown improvement in his takedown defense.
items.quote: since that fight, I feel like he's shown improvement in his takedown defense.

items.claim: CLD is difficult to hit cleanly and possesses high explosiveness.
items.quote: as far as striking goes, he's always kind of hard to hit clean and he's very explosive and he's very good.
